author: enhen3
description: Plan tasks, complete Review loops, and preview weekly, monthly, and AI profile insights from daily notes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: enhen3
manifest.description: Turn Daily Notes into a Plan → Review → Insights loop with tasks, catch-up reviews, and AI reports.

name: TideLog

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/enhen3/Tidelog/compare/1.1.47...1.1.48

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# TideLog 1.1.47

## Markdown Document Polish

- Added a unified TideLog document style for generated daily notes, weekly plans, monthly plans, insight reports, profile files, principles, patterns, quick capture, plan suggestions, and legacy-imported daily notes.
- Added TideLog callout blocks so AI-generated reports are easier to scan in Obsidian Reading View.
- Kept native Markdown task checkboxes as top-level tasks so Obsidian task parsing continues to work.
- Added regression coverage to prevent future formatting changes from blockquoting tasks or duplicating title emoji.

## Long AI Generation Reliability

- OpenAI-compatible providers now try real SSE streaming before falling back to Obsidian `requestUrl` non-streaming calls.
- Long first-insight/profile generations can show live progress instead of waiting silently on a single long request.
- Added safer fallback handling for empty or malformed stream responses so users do not receive a blank report.
- Improved handling of stream chunks that arrive without a trailing newline.

## First Insight And Profile Cleanups

- Redesigned the old-journal folder picker as a clearer desktop-style folder tree with dedicated expand/collapse controls, aligned folder rows, selected-folder labeling, and keyboard expand/collapse support.
- Hidden machine-readable profile/extraction blocks are stripped more reliably from previews and archived reports.
- First Insight report previews keep internal profile update tags hidden while the model is still streaming.
- Old-journal profile generation keeps the original source notes read-only and archives normalized copies for evidence.

## Release Hardening

- Added AI streaming regression tests.
- Updated development lockfile dependencies to clear current npm audit findings.
- Verified typecheck, API typecheck, Obsidian lint, tests, production build, release asset checks, diff whitespace checks, and npm audit.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# TideLog 1.1.46

## 首次洞察画像

- Added a first-run path that builds an initial profile from old journals.
- Imported legacy journals are copied into the TideLog archive and normalized without changing the original notes.
- The first profile report now uses five evidence-backed sections: recurring themes, repeated behavior pattern, possible blind spot, one small experiment, and evidence references.
- Users can optionally copy old journals into TideLog dated daily notes for future Plan, Review, and Insights workflows.

## Onboarding And Settings

- Added old-journal profile entry points in onboarding, Insights, and settings.
- Added privacy copy explaining that journals stay local and AI is only called when the user starts generation.
- Refined AI setup copy and provider/model defaults for a simpler first-run configuration flow.
- Removed the device-management CTA from settings.

## Generation Experience

- Added conservative time ranges for old-journal profile generation.
- Added clearer in-progress messaging near the loading button.
- Report generation now scrolls to the generated report when ready.
- The generate button now switches to a completed state after the report is ready and resets if the selected folder changes.

## Quality

- Updated profile prompts and templates so future AI profile updates use the same evidence-backed standard.
- Added regression coverage for legacy import, source-note protection, report structure, profile merge behavior, onboarding, settings, and first-insight UI states.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
# v1.1.32 — Commercial flow hardening

This patch tightens paid-product behavior before broader distribution:

- Fixes License activation error handling so invalid keys, revoked keys, and device-limit errors show the server's business message instead of being misreported as network failures.
- Retries transient License server 5xx responses before surfacing a failure to the user.
- Locks the standalone Kanban view for Free users so the product behavior matches the public Free vs Pro feature table.
- Adds commercial-flow regression tests for License API error handling and Pro gating.

Verification:

- `npm run check`
- `npm --prefix api run test:portal`
- Live License API `/health`, `/portal`, invalid activation, and invalid lookup checks
- Obsidian official plugin dashboard review status check for the previous release

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
# TideLog 1.1.31

Commercial readiness hardening release.

## Changed

- Stores AI API keys and TideLog Pro license keys in Obsidian SecretStorage instead of ordinary plugin settings data.
- Raises the minimum Obsidian app version to 1.11.4 because SecretStorage is required.
- Masks license keys in activation failure messages so users are not encouraged to paste full keys into support channels.
- Blocks command-palette weekly/monthly insight and dashboard generation when Pro is not active, matching the visible Pro UI.

## Fixed

- Accepts common mood score input like `7` or `7/10` at the end of evening review.
- Preserves the user's joy/emotion review answer instead of overwriting it with the final numeric mood score.
- Uses cryptographically secure randomness for device IDs and license key generation.
- Returns a generic 500 error from the license API instead of exposing internal exception text.
- Removes conflicting follow-up-question instructions from evening review prompts that auto-advance to the next question.

releases.description:
## TideLog 1.1.22

This release addresses the automated review failures reported for `1.1.21`.

### Fixed

- Removed `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` disable comments that are not accepted by Obsidian's automated review.
- Moved brand-name and placeholder UI strings through existing dynamic translation helpers so the source code no longer needs sentence-case rule exceptions.
- Replaced `activeWindow` timer calls with `window` timer calls to match the current Obsidian review recommendations.

### Changed

- Removed the deprecated `builtin-modules` development dependency and replaced it with Node's built-in `module.builtinModules` in the build script.
releases.name: v1.1.22

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## TideLog 1.1.21

This release improves the first-run experience and the official plugin listing readiness.

### Added

- Added a first-run onboarding modal that guides users through AI setup, the daily planning/review loop, and TideLog Pro.
- Added a `TideLog: Open getting started` command and a settings entry to reopen the guide.
- Added `fundingUrl` to `manifest.json` so Obsidian can surface the Pro purchase link.

### Improved

- Reworked the README opening sections to better explain TideLog's value, quick start path, and Free vs Pro upgrade path.
- Added regression coverage for onboarding completion persistence and onboarding UI actions.
releases.name: v1.1.21

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed the Review Daily question editor failing to open in the real Obsidian settings UI.
- Restored editing of review question names and question content from the expanded editor.

## Root cause

The previous test environment incorrectly polyfilled `document.createDiv()`. The production settings UI used `activeDocument.createDiv()` when opening the detail editor, but that helper is not reliable on `document` in Obsidian. The editor now creates the detail panel with the native document API from the clicked row, then uses normal element helpers inside the panel.

## Validation

- Removed the misleading `document.createDiv()` test polyfill.
- Added an assertion that the settings UI test environment does not provide `document.createDiv()`.
- Verified row click, triangle text-node click, title text-node click, name editing, content editing, collapse/re-expand, enable toggle, newly added question editing, and delete behavior.
- Ran the full plugin check and dependency audit before publishing this release.
releases.name: v1.1.20

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed the Review Daily question editor not expanding when clicking the triangle icon or question title in the real Obsidian/Electron UI.
- Restored editing of review question names and question text from the expanded detail panel.

## Technical note

The previous test clicked the triangle element directly, but Chromium can report the click target as the text node inside that element. The row click handler did not handle text-node targets, so it threw before opening the editor. This release adds direct triangle/title click handlers and makes the row handler text-node safe.

## Validation

- Added regression coverage for text-node clicks on the triangle and question title.
- Ran the full plugin check before publishing this release.
releases.name: v1.1.19

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed Review Daily so it respects the enabled review questions from settings.
- Fixed the review-question settings list so clicking a question row opens the editable detail panel again.
- Added an explicit enable checkbox for each review question in settings.
- Preserved existing behavior for older settings data: questions without an `enabled` field are treated as enabled.
- Fixed CI by declaring the `jsdom` test dependency used by the settings UI regression tests.

## Validation

- Added regression coverage for editing default and newly added review questions.
- Added regression coverage for disabled questions being skipped in Review Daily.
- Verified clean install, full plugin checks, production release assets, and dependency audit before publishing this release.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
# v1.1.16 — Official directory submission readiness

## Review readiness

- Replaced the MIT license with a TideLog source-available license that preserves source review while restricting redistribution and license-gate bypasses.
- Added a privacy policy covering local vault data, AI provider requests, license verification, Afdian purchase links, Cloudflare Workers logs, and D1 rate-limit counters.
- Expanded the README with Free vs Pro details, Afdian purchase flow, AI-provider account requirements, network-use disclosure, and support guidance.

## Reliability

- Removed startup vault writes and automatic view opening; TideLog now starts passive file-link listening only after Obsidian restores the workspace.
- Replaced background file writes with `Vault.process` wrappers to avoid conflicts with other plugins editing the same files.
- Added D1-backed rate limiting for license activation, verification, deactivation, portal lookup, and portal device unbinding.

## Release process

- Removed tracked build artifacts from source control and ignored generated JavaScript files.
- Split build and local deploy scripts so `npm run build` no longer writes into a personal vault path.
- Added CI and `npm run check` to run typecheck, Obsidian lint, tests, production build, and release-asset validation.

## Verification

- `npm run check`
- `npx tsc --noEmit -p api/tsconfig.json`
- `npm --prefix api run test:portal`
releases.name: v1.1.16

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
# v1.1.15 — License portal entry in settings

## Feature
Added direct entries to the License portal from the TideLog settings tab so users don't have to dig through emails to find their key or manage device bindings:

- **Pro users** — a "Manage device bindings" row is shown under the Pro status, with a button that opens the portal so the user can review or unbind old devices when activating on a new machine.
- **Free / not-yet-activated users** — a "Look up my License" row is shown above the purchase link, so users who already bought but lost their email/key can recover it via order ID + email.

Both buttons open `https://tidelog-api.mydreamchronicle.com/portal` in the system browser via `window.open()`. No additional network traffic is initiated by the plugin itself; this is consistent with the network disclosure already documented in the README.

## i18n
Added 6 new strings (3 keys × zh + en) for the new settings entries:
- `settings.manageDevices`, `settings.manageDevicesDesc`, `settings.manageDevicesBtn`
- `settings.lostCode`, `settings.lostCodeDesc`, `settings.lostCodeBtn`

## Verification
- `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` (recommended config): clean (0 errors, 0 warnings)
- `tsc --noEmit -skipLibCheck`: clean
- All four node-based test suites pass:
  - `test-bug-repro.mjs`
  - `test-evening-sop.mjs`
  - `test-save-load.mjs`
  - `test-settings-ui.mjs`
releases.name: 1.1.15 — License portal entry in settings

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## Changes

- **README**: removed redundant "Obsidian Plugin" suffix from title (reviewer requirement)
- **manifest.json**: shortened description to 73 characters
- **Vault folder creation**: moved from eager `onload()` to lazy — folders are only created when a feature (SOP, insight generation) is first used, never on bare plugin load
- **Settings**: applied `normalizePath()` to user-provided folder paths (Obsidian guideline)
- **README**: added "Network Usage" section disclosing AI API calls and Pro license verification endpoint

## Verification

- `tsc -noEmit -skipLibCheck`: clean
- `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` recommended: clean
- All test suites: pass
releases.name: v1.1.14 — Pre-submission polish

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
# v1.1.13 — Daily review customization fix

## Fix
- **Daily review now respects user-customized questions and order**. The settings UI lets users edit each evening review question's content and reorder them via drag-and-drop, but those customizations were not reliably applied when starting a Daily review.

## Root cause
Two interacting defects in `loadSettings`:
1. **Aliasing** — the shallow merge `{...DEFAULT_SETTINGS, ...saved}` left `settings.eveningQuestions` directly aliased to `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.eveningQuestions` when the user had no saved `eveningQuestions` field. Settings-UI edits then mutated the module-level defaults in place.
2. **Language-stale defaults** — `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.eveningQuestions` is computed at module load while the i18n language is still the startup default (`zh`). Users with `language: 'en'` got Chinese default questions mixed in with whatever they customized — which reads from the user's perspective as "customizations didn't take effect."

## Resolution
`loadSettings` now:
- applies the user's saved language **before** any default generation so `t()` resolves in the correct language;
- deep-clones `saved.eveningQuestions` instead of aliasing it to the defaults;
- regenerates fresh defaults via `getDefaultEveningQuestions()` when `saved.eveningQuestions` is missing or empty.

## Verification
- `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` (recommended config): clean
- `tsc --noEmit -skipLibCheck`: clean
- New test coverage: 41/41 assertions pass across three node-based test files (`test-bug-repro.mjs`, `test-evening-sop.mjs`, `test-save-load.mjs`) that exercise EveningSOP flow + save/load round-trips + before/after of both defects.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.12 — Obsidian community plugin review compliance

## Changes
- Bumped minAppVersion to 1.7.2 to match Workspace.revealLeaf requirement.
- Migrated all UI element creation to Obsidian helpers (createDiv / createSpan / createEl).
- Replaced document.* references with activeDocument.* for popout-window compatibility.
- Replaced setTimeout / clearTimeout with activeWindow.setTimeout / activeWindow.clearTimeout.
- Floating promises are now properly awaited or marked void; as TFile casts replaced with instanceof TFile guards.
- Tightened previously-any-typed values across vault-manager, license-manager, chat-controller, and pro-modal.
- Removed unused variables; replaced remaining console.log with console.debug.

## Verification
eslint-plugin-obsidianmd@0.2.9 (recommended config) and tsc --noEmit -skipLibCheck both pass with zero errors and zero warnings.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.10 — Tab navigation reset fix

## Changes
- **Tab state reset**: When switching between Plan/Trends/Review tabs, the view now always resets to the current day and month
  - Plan tab: resets to Day mode with today's date selected
  - Trends tab: resets calendar to the current month
  - Review (chat) tab: shows fresh initial state
- Only applies to user-initiated tab clicks; programmatic refreshes (e.g. after task toggle) preserve navigation context

## Bug Fixed
Previously, navigating away from a tab and returning would show the last-viewed date/month instead of the current day. This caused confusion when users completed their daily planning and switched to Trends — they would see stale data from a previous session.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.9 — Review compliance & release cleanup

## Changes
- **Sentence case compliance**: Fixed remaining casing issues (SOP abbreviation, HTTPS placeholder, I pronoun)
- **en.ts cleanup**: Proper handling of abbreviations and proper nouns
- **Release cleanup**: Removed 6 internal beta pre-releases (1.1.0-beta.1 through beta.6)

## Compliance Status
All ObsidianReviewBot Required violations resolved:
- ✅ No `fetch()` — all network via `requestUrl`
- ✅ No `innerHTML` — safe DOM APIs only
- ✅ No `as TFile` — redundant assertions removed
- ✅ No inline `style.*` — CSS classes + `setCssProps`
- ✅ Sentence case — UI text follows guidelines
- ✅ Plugin name removed from settings headings
- ✅ `authorUrl` points to author profile
- ✅ License file present

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.8 — China network fix

## Fix
- **License activation now works in China**: Switched the license API from `.workers.dev` domain (blocked in some regions) to a custom domain `tidelog-api.mydreamchronicle.com`, enabling activation without VPN.

## Improvement  
- **Better error messages**: Activation failure now shows the device ID and key, making manual activation support easier.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.7 — Activation error improvement

## Improvement
- **Better activation error message**: When license activation fails due to network issues (common in China where `.workers.dev` domains may be blocked), the error message now includes the activation key and device ID, allowing the developer to manually activate the license on the server side.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.6 — Calendar improvements & bug fix

## New Feature
- **Calendar task badges**: Past dates with incomplete tasks show a warm amber badge with the count (gentle reminder, not alarm)

## Bug Fix
- **Fix calendar month jump**: Clicking a date after navigating months no longer causes the calendar to jump to an unexpected month. Root cause was `periodicMonthOffset` not being reset when selecting a date cell.

## Improvements
- **Welcome message**: Rewritten with brand-aligned tone (no more 'AI Coach' label)
- **Pro modal i18n**: All Chinese translations for the Pro upgrade modal now display correctly

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.5 — Calendar incomplete task badges

## New Feature
- **Calendar task badges**: Past dates with incomplete tasks now show a red number badge (iOS notification style) at the top-right corner of the calendar cell
- Badge displays the exact count of unfinished tasks for that day
- Visual reminder to help users catch and handle overdue tasks

## Design Details
- Red circular badge with white number, subtle shadow for depth
- Only appears on past dates (today and future excluded)
- Optimized: uses `cachedRead` and pre-checks metadata cache before reading files

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.4 — i18n improvements & welcome message polish

## Localization
- **Fix Pro modal Chinese translations**: All `pro.*` i18n keys now have proper Chinese text (previously showed raw keys like `pro.featureTitle` in the upgrade modal)
- Added translations: feature descriptions, upgrade prompts, redemption code links

## UX Copy
- **Rewrite welcome message**: Removed generic "AI 教练" (AI coach) label — the tone is now warm and companion-like
- New welcome copy uses the TideLog brand metaphor "记录每天的潮汐涨落" (record the daily rise and fall of your tides)
- Cleaner layout with breathing whitespace for better readability

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.3 — Bug fixes, UI navigation, and review progress bar

## Bug Fixes
- **Fix Trends closure detection**: Days with only the template header (no actual review content) were incorrectly marked as review-complete
- **Fix progress bar question count**: Progress bar now correctly shows all listed questions from settings, matching the UI (previously skipped questions with a vestigial `enabled=false` flag)

## UI Improvements
- **Plan sub-tab active states**: 日/周/月 buttons show a clearly highlighted selected state (teal gradient + background)
- **Review sub-tab active states**: Daily and Insight buttons show a gold-themed active state when selected
- **Evening Review progress bar**: Segmented progress bar during Daily review showing current step, total steps, and question label (e.g. `第 1/4 步 · 成功日记~`)

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.2 — Migrate purchase channel to Aifadian

## Changes
- **Purchase channel migration**: Replaced Mianbaoduo + Gumroad dual-channel with single Aifadian (爱发电) purchase link
- **Simplified purchase UI**: All purchase buttons now use a single "🛒 购买 Pro" CTA pointing to the Aifadian product page
- **Updated i18n**: Cleaned up Chinese and English translation keys for the new purchase flow
- **Code cleanup**: Simplified `getPurchaseUrls()` → `getPurchaseUrl()` across all UI components (Pro modal, settings, dashboard, calendar, evening SOP)

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.1 — Fix view restoration on Obsidian restart

## Bug Fix
- **Fix: 'Plugin is no longer active' error on restart** — Register custom views (chat, kanban, calendar, dashboard) before any async initialization. Obsidian restores saved workspace leaves during plugin load; the previous ordering caused view factories to be registered too late, resulting in orphaned panes showing '插件不再活动' when users reopened Obsidian.

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
v1.1.0 — Stable release for Obsidian community plugin submission.

## Changes since 1.0.0
- Comprehensive sentence case compliance for all UI strings
- Error diagnostics system with TL-XXYY error codes
- Mobile-optimized touch interactions
- Settings migration for deprecated models
- Unused variable cleanup
- All Obsidian ReviewBot violations resolved

releases.author: enhen3
releases.description:
## TideLog v1.0.0

Initial release — AI-guided daily planning and reflection for Obsidian.

### Features
- Morning Plan SOP with multi-task input and subtask nesting
- Evening Review SOP (5+4 question flow)
- Weekly and monthly insight reports
- Calendar heatmap with emotion tracking
- Kanban board with day/week/month views
- Dashboard with progress tracking
- Free AI chat for coaching and reflection
- Supports OpenRouter, Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenAI, and custom providers
